Shrine buildings |所在地={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765}}{{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296}} Ōkuma Koyama, Nishiyama 21 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|4|33.1|N|140|49|39.9|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}} year 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640}}) |本殿=Single-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684}} |例祭={{ill|lt=March 28|Q2458|WD=Q2458}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまおなたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまおなたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまおなたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Shrine buildings |所在地={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} Ōkuma Koyama, Nishiyama 21 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|4|33.1|N|140|49|39.9|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) |本殿=Single-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=March 28|Q2458|WD=Q2458|1=March 28}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまおなたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまおなたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqqqqqq]]

category|Category:Kashima-Onata-jinja}} [[JA:鹿島緒名太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11677158}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kashima Onata Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja.JPG|270px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} Ōkuma Koyama, Nishiyama 21 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|4|33.1|N|140|49|39.9|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) |本殿=Single-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=March 28|Q2458|WD=Q2458|1=March 28}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かしまおなたしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Shrine buildings |所在地={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} Ōkuma Koyama, Nishiyama 21 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|4|33.1|N|140|49|39.9|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) |本殿=Single-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=March 28|Q2458|WD=Q2458|1=March 28}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:宮城県の神社]] [[Category:宮城県の旧村社]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine

The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. category|Category:Kashima-Onata-jinja}} [[JA:鹿島緒名太神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11677158}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Kashima Onata Shrine |画像=[[File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja.JPG|270px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} Ōkuma Koyama, Nishiyama 21 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|4|33.1|N|140|49|39.9|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) |本殿=Single-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=March 28|Q2458|WD=Q2458|1=March 28}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:陸奥国の式内社]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Shrine buildings |所在地={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} Ōkuma Koyama, Nishiyama 21 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|4|33.1|N|140|49|39.9|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) |本殿=Single-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=March 28|Q2458|WD=Q2458|1=March 28}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:鹿島神社]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:qqqqqqq]]

Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine

The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. Shrine buildings |所在地={{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}{{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}} Ōkuma Koyama, Nishiyama 21 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|38|4|33.1|N|140|49|39.9|E|region:JP-04_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=village shrine|Q134917284|WD=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |創建={{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 41 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|WD=Q30640|1=AD 111}}) |本殿=Single-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |例祭={{ill|lt=March 28|Q2458|WD=Q2458|1=March 28}} |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Miyagi|label=Kashima
Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]] [[Category:pages]]

Onata Shrine|position=top|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kashima Onata Shrine''' (かしまおなたじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|Q47896|WD=Q47896|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} {{ill|lt=Watari|Q1205296|WD=Q1205296|1=Watari, Miyagi}}. Its {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=former shrine ranking|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was village shrine. It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|WD=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|WD=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|WD=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|WD=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|WD=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
Passing through a grove of cedar trees File:Haiden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine 2.JPG|Haiden of Kashima Onata Shrine seen from the approach stairs File:Honden of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Single-bay nagare-zukuri honden of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Precincts of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Precincts of Kashima Onata Shrine *On the north side of the shrine buildings, stone shrines and stone monuments stand in a row. The stone shrine closest to the main building has a chrysanthemum crest carved on it. File:Small shrines and steles of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Stone shrines and stone monuments of Kashima Onata Shrine File:Small shrines of Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|The stone shrine on the left has an eight-petal chrysanthemum crest carved on it === References === *''Watari-gun Shrine Registry'' (Meiji 32 [1899], held at Kawaguchi Shrine) === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === {{commonscat|Kashima-Onata-jinja}} * [https://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310010279 Kashima Onata Shrine] (Miyagi Prefecture {{ill|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines|Q11590703|WD=Q11590703|1=Jinjachō}}) {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kashima Onata Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha in Miyagi Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Mutsu Province]] [[Category:亘理町の歴史]] [[Category:Kashima Shrines]] [[Category:亘理町の建築物]]

It is one of the four {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} listed in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} for {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|qid=Q1131765|1=Watari District, Miyagi}}. === Enshrined deity === ; {{ill|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|1=Takemikazuchi}} : There is a theory that the name of Kashima Onata Shrine, like {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|qid=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is derived from the name of Onata-no-kami, a child deity of Takemikazuchi. === History === According to shrine tradition, the shrine was established by imperial decree on the 6th day of the 8th month in the 41st year of {{ill|lt=Emperor Keikō|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. It is designated as a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, and in ancient times the entire Koyama district was shrine territory. Later, during the wars of the {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} era, the shrine lands fell into disuse. In {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 5 (1872), the shrine was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=village shrine|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Kashima Onata Shrine, together with {{ill|lt=Kashima Amatarishiwake Shrine|Q11677110|qid=Q11677110|1=Kashima Amatarashi Wake Shrine}}, is considered to be one of the two shrines enshrined in {{ill|lt=Watari District|Q1131765|qid=Q1131765|lt=Watari District|1=Watari District, Miyagi}} among the "thirty-eight shrines enshrining descendant deities of the great Kashima deity in Mutsu Province" recorded in the "{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}." === Precincts === *Shrine buildings (haiden, heiden, honden) File:Approach to Kashima Onata-jinja shrine.JPG|Approach to Kashima Onata Shrine
